title: Title 5 System Inspector license SI#3924 (Arthur Bloomquist) — issue date + training history

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Title 5 System Inspector license SI#3924 (Arthur Bloomquist) — issue date + training history
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

To the MassDEP Records Access Officer,

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, M.G.L. c.66 §10, I am requesting the following public records held by the Massachusetts Department of Environmental Protection:

**Records sought:**

For the Title 5 System Inspector license **SI#3924**, issued to **Arthur G. Bloomquist** of Plympton, MA (currently or formerly d/b/a "Arthur Bloomquist LLC" and "Title V Inspections"):

1. The **original issue date** of license SI#3924.

2. The **complete renewal history** of license SI#3924 from issuance through the most recent renewal cycle (records indicate a final renewal cycle running through 6/30/2019).

3. Any **Continuing Education Unit (CEU)** completion records, training records, or course-attendance records on file at MassDEP for license-holder Arthur G. Bloomquist, including:
   - Course name and provider
   - Date completed
   - Sponsoring entity or paying entity, if recorded

4. Any **initial Title 5 System Inspector training certification** documents (the qualifying training that preceded initial license issuance), including the training provider, dates of attendance, and any sponsoring or paying entity recorded in the application file.

5. Any **soil evaluator** or **Title 5 inspector approval** application materials submitted by Arthur G. Bloomquist to MassDEP that reference a sponsoring board, town, employer, or paying agency.

**Public-interest predicate:**

The records bear directly on a contested Plympton Board of Health election held May 16, 2026, in which the incumbent Chairman publicly stated that the challenger had "use[d] the board to have the town pay for my training to start a private business when I leave." The training records and their funding source are the records that test that public claim.

**Preferred format:** Electronic delivery (PDF) via email.

**Fee waiver:** Fees waived under M.G.L. c.66 §10(d)(iv) — public-interest disclosure.

**Response window:** Ten business days, per M.G.L. c.66 §10(b).

Thank you.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey

title: Failed Title 5 Systems list, all versions 2017-2026

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Failed Title 5 Systems list, all versions 2017-2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Members of the Plympton Board of Health,

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, M.G.L. c.66 §10, I am requesting:

**Records sought:**

1. Every version of the "Failed Systems" list maintained by the Plympton Board of Health from **December 1, 2017** (date of the oldest entry currently appearing on the May 12, 2026 BoH agenda — 50 Forest Street) to the present.

2. For each property currently or historically appearing on the Failed Systems list:
   - Property address and Assessor's Map / Block / Lot ID
   - Date the failed Title 5 inspection report was filed
   - Name of the inspector who filed the report
   - Property owner of record at the time of filing
   - Subsequent property-owner changes, if any
   - Any compliance correspondence, enforcement orders, or extensions issued by the Board of Health or the Health Agent
   - Current status (open, resolved, abandoned)
   - Date of resolution if closed

3. Specifically, the full file for **50 Forest Street, Assessor's Map 1 Block 5 Lot 8** (failure report dated December 1, 2017), and for **36 Colchester Street, Assessor's Map 18 Block 2 Lot 4** (failure report dated June 6, 2025), and for **50 Maple Street, Map 7 Block 2 Lot 11**, and **36 Parsonage Road, Map 17 Block 1 Lot 1**.

**Preferred format:** Electronic delivery (PDF) via email.

**Fee waiver:** Fees waived under M.G.L. c.66 §10(d)(iv) and 950 CMR 32.07(2)(k) — public-interest disclosure. The records concern the Board of Health's enforcement record on failed Title 5 systems, including one entry on the active list for eight years and five months. The public interest is heightened by Article 19 of the May 13, 2026 ATM warrant, which proposes to move the BoH's only staff position out from under its appointing authority.

**Response window:** Ten business days, per M.G.L. c.66 §10(b).

Thank you.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey

title: 2007 Board of Health minutes referencing Arthur G. Bloomquist property variance

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: 2007 Board of Health minutes referencing Arthur G. Bloomquist property variance
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Town Clerk Shaw,

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, M.G.L. c.66 §10, I am requesting the following public records held by the Town of Plympton:

**Records sought:**

1. All **Plympton Board of Health meeting minutes**, agenda postings, and supporting submission documents from **January 1, 2007 through December 31, 2008** that reference, mention, or pertain to a septic system plan, variance, permit, or Title 5 matter on any property owned or occupied by **Arthur G. Bloomquist**, including but not limited to:
   - 96 Lake Street, Plympton
   - 109 West Street, Plympton
   - 115 West Street, Plympton

2. Any **variance decision document**, **conditions of approval**, or **applicant request letter** in the BoH file pertaining to the above property and time window.

3. The **promulgation file** for any Plympton Board of Health regulation amendment in effect during 2007 that governed mounded or raised on-site sewage disposal systems, including the hearing notice (M.G.L. c.111 §27), hearing minutes, and Attorney General certification.

**Predicate for this request:**

On May 16, 2026, sitting Plympton Board of Health Chairman Bradford Cronin publicly stated, in writing in the Facebook group "Plympton Helping Plympton — USA," that he has "hard evidence, like meeting minutes from 2007 for a variance being issued on his [Bloomquist's] property at the owner's request." Chairman Cronin's public assertion that these specific records exist defeats any "no responsive records" response under M.G.L. c.66 §10(b). I am requesting the records he describes.

**Preferred format:** Electronic delivery (PDF) via email. Paper inspection at the Town Clerk's office is also acceptable.

**Fee waiver:** Fees waived under M.G.L. c.66 §10(d)(iv) — public-interest disclosure. The records bear directly on a contested Board of Health election and a public dispute concerning the proper promulgation of town health regulations.

**Response window:** Ten business days, per M.G.L. c.66 §10(b).

Thank you.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey

title: BoH Administrative Assistant personnel and appointment history

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: BoH Administrative Assistant personnel and appointment history
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Town Clerk Shaw,

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, M.G.L. c.66 §10, I am requesting the following public records held by the Town of Plympton:

**Records sought:**

1. The current job description for the position titled "Administrative Assistant to the Board of Health" in the Town of Plympton.

2. The original appointment letter or vote of appointment for Cathy Ferguson to the position of Administrative Assistant to the Board of Health, including the date of appointment and the appointing body.

3. The complete list of every individual who has held the position of Administrative Assistant to the Board of Health in Plympton from January 1, 2015 to the present, with appointment dates, departure dates, and the appointing body at the time of each appointment.

4. Any personnel actions (hire, promotion, evaluation, discipline, separation) on file regarding the Administrative Assistant to the Board of Health from January 1, 2015 to the present, redacted as required by M.G.L. c.4 §7(26)(c) but with public-facing employment actions identified by date and category.

5. The salary and benefit history (Wage & Personnel Classification Plan placement) for the Administrative Assistant to the Board of Health from FY2020 to present.

**Preferred format:** Electronic delivery (PDF) via email.

**Fee waiver:** I respectfully request a waiver of any fees under M.G.L. c.66 §10(d)(iv) and 950 CMR 32.07(2)(k). The records concern the position whose appointing authority is the subject of Article 19 of the May 13, 2026 Annual Town Meeting warrant — a matter of significant public interest given the contested Board of Health election on May 16, 2026.

**Response window:** Per M.G.L. c.66 §10(b), I expect a response within ten business days. If any portion is withheld for personal-privacy reasons under M.G.L. c.4 §7(26)(c), please redact narrowly and identify the categories of information withheld.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey

title: Selectmen / Board of Health correspondence Jan 1 – May 13, 2026

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Selectmen / Board of Health correspondence Jan 1 – May 13, 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Town Clerk Shaw,

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, M.G.L. c.66 §10, I am requesting the following public records held by the Town of Plympton:

**Records sought:** All electronic correspondence (emails, text messages on town-issued devices, and Microsoft Teams / Zoom chat records) sent or received between **January 1, 2026 and May 13, 2026** in which any of the following appear as a sender, recipient, cc'd party, or named subject:

- Dana Smith (Selectmen, Chairman)
- Nathaniel B. Sides (Selectmen, Vice Chairman)
- The vacant third Selectman position (any incoming inquiries or candidate communications)
- Bradford Cronin (Board of Health, Chairman)
- Jared Anderson (Board of Health, Vice Chair/Treasurer)
- Arthur B. Morin, Jr. (Board of Health, Clerk)
- Cathy Ferguson (Administrative Assistant to the Board of Health)
- Kevin Forgue (Health Agent)
- Liz Dennehy (former Town Administrator, departed March 2026)
- Bob Fennessy (Interim Town Administrator, from approximately April 27, 2026)

**AND** in which any of the following search terms appear:

- "Article 19"
- "Administrative Assistant"
- "Section 27" or "§27"
- "Chapter 111" or "c.111"
- "special legislation"
- "appointing authority"
- "Board of Health"
- "abolish" or "abolished"

**Preferred format:** Electronic delivery (PDF or native format) via email to the address above, or via the Plympton public records portal if available.

**Fee waiver:** I respectfully request a waiver of any fees, as production of these records is in the public interest. M.G.L. c.66 §10(d)(iv) and 950 CMR 32.07(2)(k) authorize fee waivers where disclosure primarily benefits the public rather than a commercial interest. The records concern the appointing authority over public-health staff in the Town of Plympton — a matter of significant public interest given Article 19 of the May 13, 2026 Annual Town Meeting warrant. Three days before a contested Board of Health election, the public is entitled to understand the deliberative record behind the petition.

**Response window:** Per M.G.L. c.66 §10(b), I expect a response within ten business days. If any portion is withheld, please cite the specific exemption claimed under M.G.L. c.4 §7(26) and identify the responsive records being withheld so that I may evaluate any appeal.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey

title: Building permit A1348 application file — 27/29 Manomet Point Road

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Building permit A1348 application file — 27/29 Manomet Point Road
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to M.G.L. c. 66 §10, I respectfully request the following public records from the Plymouth Building Department (forwarding as needed via the Town Clerk):

1. The complete application file for building permit number A1348 issued for work at 27 and/or 29 Manomet Point Road (the Joseph Simes House), with a stated valuation of $2,600,000, issued in or about 2014 and active through approximately 2017.
2. The name and address of the general contractor and any named subcontractors listed on the permit application.
3. All inspection records, certificates of occupancy, and progress reports associated with this permit.
4. Any amendments, change orders, or supplemental permits referencing the same property and the same project.

If any portion is exempt, please identify the specific exemption claimed and produce the segregable portions. Per M.G.L. c. 66 §10, please respond within 10 business days.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey
